speaker: CORDELIA
text: O, thou good Kent, how shall I live and work

speaker: CORDELIA
text: To match thy goodness? My life will be too short,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: And every measure fail me.

speaker: KENT
text: To be acknowledged, madam, is o’erpaid.

speaker: KENT
text: All my reports go with the modest truth,

speaker: KENT
text: Nor more, nor clipped, but so.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Be better suited.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: These weeds are memories of those worser hours.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: I prithee put them off.

speaker: KENT
text: Pardon, dear madam.

speaker: KENT
text: Yet to be known shortens my made intent.

speaker: KENT
text: My boon I make it that you know me not

speaker: KENT
text: Till time and I think meet.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Then be ’t so, my good lord.—How does the King?

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Madam, sleeps still.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: O, you kind gods,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Cure this great breach in his abusèd nature!

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Th’ untuned and jarring senses, O, wind up,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Of this child-changèd father!

speaker: DOCTOR
text: So please your Majesty

speaker: DOCTOR
text: That we may wake the King? He hath slept long.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Be governed by your knowledge, and proceed

speaker: CORDELIA
text: I’ th’ sway of your own will. Is he arrayed?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Ay, madam. In the heaviness of sleep,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: We put fresh garments on him.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Be by, good madam, when we do awake him.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: I doubt not of his temperance.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Very well.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Please you, draw near.—Louder the music there.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: O, my dear father, restoration hang

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Thy medicine on my lips, and let this kiss

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Repair those violent harms that my two sisters

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Have in thy reverence made.

speaker: KENT
text: Kind and dear princess.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Had you not been their father, these white flakes

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Did challenge pity of them. Was this a face

speaker: CORDELIA
text: To be opposed against the jarring winds?

speaker: CORDELIA
text: To stand against the deep dread-bolted thunder,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: In the most terrible and nimble stroke

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Of quick cross-lightning? To watch, poor perdu,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: With this thin helm? Mine enemy’s dog,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Though he had bit me, should have stood that night

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Against my fire. And wast thou fain, poor father,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: To hovel thee with swine and rogues forlorn

speaker: CORDELIA
text: In short and musty straw? Alack, alack,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: ’Tis wonder that thy life and wits at once

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Had not concluded all.—He wakes. Speak to him.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Madam, do you; ’tis fittest.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: How does my royal lord? How fares your Majesty?

speaker: LEAR
text: You do me wrong to take me out o’ th’ grave.

speaker: LEAR
text: Thou art a soul in bliss, but I am bound

speaker: LEAR
text: Upon a wheel of fire, that mine own tears

speaker: LEAR
text: Do scald like molten lead.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Sir, do you know me?

speaker: LEAR
text: You are a spirit, I know. Where did you die?

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Still, still, far wide.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: He’s scarce awake. Let him alone awhile.

speaker: LEAR
text: Where have I been? Where am I? Fair daylight?

speaker: LEAR
text: I am mightily abused; I should e’en die with pity

speaker: LEAR
text: To see another thus. I know not what to say.

speaker: LEAR
text: I will not swear these are my hands. Let’s see.

speaker: LEAR
text: I feel this pinprick. Would I were assured

speaker: LEAR
text: Of my condition!

speaker: CORDELIA
text: O, look upon me, sir,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: And hold your hand in benediction o’er me.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: No, sir, you must not kneel.

speaker: LEAR
text: Pray do not mock:

speaker: LEAR
text: I am a very foolish fond old man,

speaker: LEAR
text: Fourscore and upward, not an hour more nor less,

speaker: LEAR
text: And to deal plainly,

speaker: LEAR
text: I fear I am not in my perfect mind.

speaker: LEAR
text: Methinks I should know you and know this man,

speaker: LEAR
text: Yet I am doubtful, for I am mainly ignorant

speaker: LEAR
text: What place this is, and all the skill I have

speaker: LEAR
text: Remembers not these garments; nor I know not

speaker: LEAR
text: Where I did lodge last night. Do not laugh at me,

speaker: LEAR
text: For, as I am a man, I think this lady

speaker: LEAR
text: To be my child Cordelia.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: And so I am; I am.

speaker: LEAR
text: Be your tears wet? Yes, faith. I pray, weep not.

speaker: LEAR
text: If you have poison for me, I will drink it.

speaker: LEAR
text: I know you do not love me, for your sisters

speaker: LEAR
text: Have, as I do remember, done me wrong.

speaker: LEAR
text: You have some cause; they have not.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: No cause, no cause.

speaker: LEAR
text: Am I in France?

speaker: KENT
text: In your own kingdom, sir.

speaker: LEAR
text: Do not abuse me.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Be comforted, good madam. The great rage,

speaker: DOCTOR
text: You see, is killed in him, and yet it is danger

speaker: DOCTOR
text: To make him even o’er the time he has lost.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Desire him to go in. Trouble him no more

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Till further settling.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Will ’t please your Highness walk?

speaker: LEAR
text: You must bear with me.

speaker: LEAR
text: Pray you now, forget, and forgive. I am old and foolish.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Holds it true, sir, that the Duke of Cornwall

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: was so slain?

speaker: KENT
text: Most certain, sir.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Who is conductor of his people?

speaker: KENT
text: As ’tis said, the bastard son of Gloucester.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: They say Edgar, his banished son, is with

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: the Earl of Kent in Germany.

speaker: KENT
text: Report is changeable. ’Tis time to look about.

speaker: KENT
text: The powers of the kingdom approach apace.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: The arbitrament is like to be bloody. Fare

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: you well, sir.

speaker: KENT
text: My point and period will be throughly wrought,

speaker: KENT
text: Or well, or ill, as this day’s battle’s fought.
